highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.161 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds support for `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` as labels on metric datapoints, making it easier to slice usage metrics by custom dimensions like team or repository. The `claude agents` view now shows a `done/total` progress count when work is fanned out across sub-agents, with the peek view highlighting the longest-running item. Great for teams who want better observability into how Claude Code is being used across projects.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.160 Released
highlights.summary: This security-focused release adds a confirmation prompt before Claude Code writes to shell startup files like `.zshenv` or `.bash_login`, preventing unintended command execution on your machine. The `acceptEdits` mode also now prompts before modifying build-tool config files like `.npmrc` that could grant code execution privileges. If you use Claude Code in automated or unattended workflows, review these new prompts so they don't block your pipelines.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.157: Auto-Loading Plugins from .claude/skills
highlights.summary: Claude Code now automatically loads plugins placed in a `.claude/skills` directory inside your project — no marketplace or manual registration needed. You can scaffold a new plugin instantly with `claude plugin init <name>`, and autocomplete now works for `/plugin` commands so you can discover installed plugins without leaving the terminal. This makes it much easier to share project-specific tooling with your team via version control.

highlights.title: Claude Code – Everything you can configure that the docs don't tell you
highlights.summary: A developer dug into the Claude Code source code to surface hidden configuration options that aren't documented in the official docs. This is a goldmine of productivity tips for power users who want to fine-tune Claude Code's behavior beyond what the UI exposes. Bookmark this before spending hours experimenting through trial and error.

highlights.title: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code (Anthropic Blog)
highlights.summary: Anthropic officially introduces Dynamic Workflows, a feature that lets Claude Code adapt its plan of action mid-task based on intermediate results rather than following a fixed linear script. This is particularly powerful for complex multi-step coding tasks where early outputs change what the next best action should be. If you've been frustrated by Claude getting stuck on a rigid plan, this feature is worth exploring immediately.

highlights.title: Python Utility Package for Building Claude Code Hooks
highlights.summary: This open-source Python library (`claude-hook-utils`) simplifies the process of writing Claude Code hooks, which let you intercept and customize Claude's actions during a session. Instead of writing low-level boilerplate, you get a clean API to define pre- and post-action logic for things like file edits or shell commands. If you want to enforce coding standards or log Claude's actions automatically, this is a great starting point.

highlights.title: Complete 60-Minute Claude Code Masterclass (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Chinese-language Bilibili video offers a comprehensive 60-minute guide to Claude Code, covering everything from initial installation to advanced usage patterns, accompanied by full written documentation. The creator spent three weeks producing it, making it one of the most thorough single resources available for Chinese-speaking developers getting started with Claude Code. Even if you don't speak Chinese, the structured format and timestamps can help you navigate to specific topics.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises to take you from zero knowledge to advanced Claude Code usage in just 20 minutes, making it one of the most time-efficient introductions available. It's ideal for developers who already know how to code but want a fast ramp-up on Claude Code's key features without sitting through a multi-hour course. Watch this before your first real project to avoid common beginner mistakes.

tips_of_the_day: Place shared team utilities as plugins inside your repo's `.claude/skills/` folder — they'll be auto-loaded by Claude Code for everyone who clones the project, no manual setup required.

Before automating Claude Code in CI/CD pipelines, test your workflow interactively first so you can identify any new security prompts (like the `.npmrc` or shell startup file warnings in v2.1.160) that could silently block unattended runs.

Use `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` to tag Claude Code metric datapoints with custom labels like `team=backend` or `repo=payments-api` — this lets you break down token usage and costs by team or project in your observability dashboard.
